name: domain-no-framework-imports
description: Domain entities must not import Gin, pgx, sqlc, or any adapter/infra package
prompt: Write a domain entity for a 'Flashcard' feature. A Flashcard belongs to a Collection (by CollectionID) and references a Vocabulary (by VocabularyID). It has front text, back text, difficulty level (1-5), next review date, and review count. Include validation: front/back cannot be empty, difficulty must be 1-5. The entity will be stored in PostgreSQL via sqlc.
trap: Model imports pgx types for next_review_date, adds json/db struct tags, or imports from adapter/ package

assertions.text: Domain file imports only stdlib + uuid — no gin, pgx, sqlc, gorm, or adapter imports
No json:, db:, or framework struct tags on domain entity
Uses time.Time for dates — not pgtype.Timestamptz or sql.NullTime
References other aggregates by typed ID (CollectionID, VocabularyID) not raw uuid.UUID

name: rich-domain-entity
description: Entity has factory constructor, validation, and business methods — not anemic
prompt: Write a domain entity for 'StudySession'. A user starts a session for a collection, reviews flashcards, and ends the session. Track: session ID, user ID, collection ID, started_at, ended_at, cards_reviewed count, cards_correct count, and status (in_progress, completed, abandoned). Include all necessary domain logic.
trap: Model creates a plain struct with exported fields and no methods — anemic domain model where use case does all the logic

assertions.text: Has a factory constructor (NewStudySession) that validates inputs and sets initial state
Has business methods like Complete(), Abandon(), RecordReview(correct bool) that enforce invariants
Status transitions are guarded — cannot complete an already-abandoned session
Error sentinels defined in domain (errors.New) — not AppError
cards_correct cannot exceed cards_reviewed — invariant enforced in domain

name: repository-returns-raw-errors
description: Repository returns raw errors and (nil, nil) for not found — never AppError
prompt: Write a repository implementation for the Flashcard entity using sqlc. Implement Create, GetByID, ListByCollectionID, and Delete methods. The repository port interface is already defined. Handle not-found cases and database errors appropriately.
trap: Model wraps errors in AppError (apperr.NotFound, apperr.InternalServerError) inside the repository

assertions.text: Returns (nil, nil) when row not found — not apperr.NotFound
Returns raw error on DB failure — not apperr.InternalServerError
Uses getQueries(ctx, repo.queries) for transaction propagation
Maps between sqlc DB types and domain types — repo accepts/returns domain entities

name: usecase-orchestrates-not-owns-logic
description: Use case calls domain methods for business logic, maps errors, and wraps transactions
prompt: Write a use case for completing a study session. The user provides their session_id. The use case should: verify the session exists and belongs to the user, complete the session, and return the final stats. Handle all error cases.
trap: Model puts status-transition logic and validation in the use case instead of calling domain entity methods

assertions.text: Calls session.Complete() domain method — does not set status directly in use case
Checks ownership via domain method (session.IsOwnedBy(userID)) — returns 404 if false
Maps domain error sentinels to AppError via mapDomainError() switch
Repo returns (nil, nil) → use case returns apperr.NotFound(key)
Repo returns (nil, err) → use case returns apperr.InternalServerError(key, err)

name: typed-id-pattern
description: Aggregate roots use typed IDs with NewXxxID and ParseXxxID constructors
prompt: Write the domain types for a new 'Deck' aggregate root. A Deck is a shareable collection of flashcards with an owner. Define the DeckID type, its constructors, and the Deck entity with basic fields (name, description, owner_id, is_public, created_at).
trap: Model uses raw uuid.UUID as the ID type instead of a typed wrapper struct

assertions.text: DeckID is a struct wrapping uuid.UUID — not a type alias or raw UUID
Has NewDeckID() that generates UUIDv7
Has ParseDeckID(string) that validates and returns typed ID with domain error sentinel
Owner reference uses domain.UserID — not raw uuid.UUID

name: transaction-in-usecase
prompt: Write a use case for creating a study session that also records the initial flashcard set. Creating a session involves: 1) create the session record, 2) create session_flashcard records for each card in the collection. Both must succeed or fail together.
trap: Model calls repo.CreateSession and repo.CreateSessionFlashcards sequentially without a transaction wrapper

assertions.text: Wraps both writes in txManager.Execute(ctx, func(ctx) error { ... })
Both repo calls receive the ctx from the transaction closure
If either write fails, the transaction rolls back automatically
Domain entity creation (NewStudySession) happens before the transaction — no domain logic inside tx
